Use Python 3.8 environment (e.g., verify with python --version) to ensure compatibility context
Install TensorFlow Model Garden official package version 2.12.0 using pip: pip install tf-models-official==2.12.0
Use the Docker image tensorflow/tensorflow:2.12.0-gpu-jupyter or equivalent environment with TensorFlow 2.12.0 installed
Clone the TensorFlow models repository from https://github.com/tensorflow/models.git
Navigate to the object detection tutorial directory or set up a script replicating the object detection training pipeline as per https://www.tensorflow.org/tfmodels/vision/object_detection tutorial
Import required modules including tensorflow as tf and tfm.core.train_lib from tf-models-official
Construct or load the experiment configuration (exp_config) according to the tutorial, ensuring parameters are consistent with the example
Set up distribution_strategy and task objects as per the tutorial's training and evaluation example script
Define model_dir as a writable directory for model checkpoints and logs

Run the training and evaluation by executing the function call: model, eval_logs = tfm.core.train_lib.run_experiment(distribution_strategy=distribution_strategy, task=task, mode='train_and_eval', params=exp_config, model_dir=model_dir, run_post_eval=True)
Observe the runtime error during evaluation phase, specifically a TypeError at line 442 in base_trainer.py in eval_step with message: unsupported operand type(s) for |: 'dict' and 'dict'
Confirm that the error occurs because Python 3.8 does not support the dict union operator '|' introduced in Python 3.9
Assert that the error traceback points to the line: return passthrough_logs | logs in base_trainer.py
End test confirming that the TypeError is reproducible in this environment with tf-models-official==2.12.0 and Python 3.8
